initial_state_overrides.draft_content:
Conquered Mission Peak at the crack of dawn and the Bay Area opened up below me like a living map — the Bay shimmering silver under the early light, the bridges strung like necklaces in the distance. 🌅

The trail is no joke: six miles round trip, 2,100 feet of elevation gain, with a final scramble up a steep rocky ridge to reach the pole. I left the trailhead at 5:30am and shared the summit with exactly three other people before the weekend runners arrived. That silence at the top, with just the wind and the view stretching from Mount Tam to the Santa Cruz mountains, made every burning step completely worth it.

Pro tips: start early (really early), bring poles for the final stretch, and pack at least two liters of water — there's no refill on trail. The parking lot fills fast on weekends, so aim for a 5am arrival or take the free shuttle from the BART station during busy months.

#MissionPeak #BayAreaHiking #EastBay #HikingBayArea #Fremont #SunriseHike #HikingCommunity

location_info.style_context: Mission Peak Regional Preserve, Fremont, CA. 6-mile round trip trail, 2,100 ft elevation gain. Steep rocky summit scramble. No water on trail. Free shuttle available from BART on weekends. Parking fills fast. Best at sunrise.
